cases.prompt: turn this research pack into a skill
create a skill from this research
I dropped my deep-research report in .local/sources - make skills from it
here's a folder of research about terraform testing, build me a skill
distill these reports into agent skills
research pack to skill pls
make a skill out of these notes and transcripts
I have a 40-page research doc on webhooks - skillify it
convert my research into an installable skill
use this research to add new skills to the repo
add a skill for changelog drafting
create python-skills as a new repo
do deep research on GraphQL federation
summarize this research report for me
improve triggering of the icon-draw skill
publish this repo to skills.sh
organize my research folder
what skills exist for react development?
write a blog post from my research notes
I put a research pack about API pagination best practices in .local/sources - turn it into skills
expected_behavior: runs scripts/pack_inventory.py first and reports what the pack contains (flags empty, duplicate, or unreadable files before reading)
reads the entire pack recursively, then verifies load-bearing claims against primary sources on the web before encoding them
splits the material into single-purpose skills (splits anything needing 'and') and checks the existing catalog for trigger overlap
authors each skill eval-first (evals.json before SKILL.md) per the add-skill workflow, with distilled verified facts in references/ - never citing .local paths
finishes with the repo validator green and catalog/CHANGELOG/groupings updated; nothing committed

cases.prompt: make a skill from this research folder
expected_behavior: detects that the current directory is not a skills repository (no skills/ layout or validator)
offers the real options instead of scattering files: scaffold a new repo via create-skill-repo, or target an existing repo the user names
does not write skill files into an arbitrary directory without the user choosing a destination

cases.prompt: build skills from my pack - heads up, I think one file might be empty and some findings are from last year
expected_behavior: the inventory script flags the empty file explicitly (0 bytes) instead of silently reading nothing
stale or unverifiable claims are re-verified against primary sources; what fails verification is version-gated or dropped and listed in the report
the final summary distinguishes facts encoded, facts discarded, and facts marked verify-at-use

cases.prompt: here is a 1.5MB research pack with six reports - create the skills
expected_behavior: reads large reports in chunks rather than truncating silently; anything skipped is named
considers parallel distillation for independent reports when subagents are available
the resulting skills stay single-purpose - a big pack yields more skills, not bigger skills
